name: retraction-watch-mcp
description: Local, privacy-friendly Retraction Watch screening with conservative evidence grading.
long_description: A local MCP server for screening names, institutions, DOI and PMID values against a local SQLite index built from the public Crossref Retraction Watch CSV. It is designed for cautious research-integrity triage, not identity adjudication or misconduct determination.

tools.name: screen_person
tools.description: Screen one person against the local Retraction Watch index.

tools.name: screen_batch
tools.description: Screen multiple people against the local Retraction Watch index.

tools.name: lookup_record
tools.description: Return one Retraction Watch record by Record ID.

tools.name: lookup_doi
tools.description: Return records whose DOI exactly matches the provided DOI.

tools.name: explain_match
tools.description: Explain how one query scores against one record.

tools.name: get_source_versions
tools.description: Return local data source and policy metadata.

prompts.name: screen-author
prompts.description: Prepare a conservative Retraction Watch author-screening workflow.
prompts.text: Use the Retraction Watch MCP tools to screen ${arguments.name}. Institution: ${arguments.institution}. DOI: ${arguments.doi}. Do not describe any non-DOI/PMID result as an identity or misconduct confirmation.

prompts.name: review-match-result
prompts.description: Turn screening JSON into a cautious human-review summary.
prompts.text: Review this Retraction Watch MCP result and produce a cautious manual-review summary. Do not state that a person committed misconduct. Result JSON: ${arguments.result_json}

prompts.name: batch-integrity-check
prompts.description: Prepare a cautious batch screening workflow.
prompts.text: Plan a cautious batch Retraction Watch screening workflow for: ${arguments.input_description}. Separate DOI/PMID exact hits from name or institution similarities.

prompts.name: explain-limitations
prompts.description: Explain what the server can and cannot establish.
prompts.text: Explain the limitations of Retraction Watch MCP: no source email field, no author-affiliation one-to-one mapping, DOI/PMID exact matches are record evidence, name-only matches require manual review, and no_match does not prove absence.

db_path.title: Retraction Watch SQLite database
db_path.description: Path to the SQLite database generated by rw-import.

policy.title: Matching policy
policy.description: Use balanced for normal screening or strict to allow only DOI/PMID exact matches as formal matches.
